items.name: Mark Twain Hall
items.notes: Located near Peace Park, the College of Engineering, and the Missouri School of Journalism. One of the most popular freshman halls. The MARK on 5th Street all-you-care-to-eat dining hall is in the building.

items.name: Wolpers Hall
items.notes: Located directly across from MizzouRec with immediate access to the MU Student Center and Student Health Center. Central campus location makes it easy to walk everywhere. Named after former Board of Curators president John Wolpers.

items.name: Johnston Hall
items.notes: Women-only hall right in the heart of campus, close to MizzouRec, the MU Student Center, and the Student Health Center. Named after Eva Johnston, the university's first dean of women students. Savor Kitchen allergen-free restaurant is in the building.

items.name: North Hall
items.notes: Suite-style rooms near Faurot Field (Memorial Stadium) and Greek Town. Convenient access to the Southwest dining hall (Restaurants at Southwest). Great for students who want a social atmosphere near the stadium.

items.name: Center Hall
items.notes: Women-only suite-style hall located near Faurot Field and Greek Town. Suite-mates share a full restroom. Close to the Restaurants at Southwest dining hall. Open during academic breaks.

items.name: Lucile Bluford Hall
items.notes: Named to honor Lucile Bluford, renowned journalist and publisher of the Kansas City Call. Community and suite-style rooms available. Located near Faurot Field, Greek Town, and the Southwest dining hall.

items.name: College Avenue Hall
items.notes: Overlooks the historic Dairy Lawn. Easy access to the College of Veterinary Medicine and Buck's Ice Cream. Suite-mates share a full restroom. Open during academic breaks.

items.name: Mizzou at U Centre
items.notes: Apartment-style housing reserved for upperclassmen. Features private bedrooms and a full kitchen — a step up from traditional dorm living. Ideal for juniors and seniors who want more independence.
